channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Israel and Iran tentatively suspend attacks against each other
item.description: Israel and Iran agree to stop strikes for now as President Trump says there's a "good chance" for a deal with Tehran in the coming days.
item.content:encoded: Israel and Iran agree to stop strikes for now as President Trump says there's a "good chance" for a deal with Tehran in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: What leverage does Trump have to put pressure on Netanyahu to quell conflict?
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks David Schenker of the Washington Institute for Near East Policy about President Trump's leverage in dealings with Israel's prime minister.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel asks David Schenker of the Washington Institute for Near East Policy about President Trump's leverage in dealings with Israel's prime minister.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Trump formally nominates Todd Blanche to serve as attorney general
item.description: President Trump officially nominated Todd Blanche to be the next Attorney General, setting up a potential confirmation fight in Congress.
item.content:encoded: President Trump officially nominated Todd Blanche to be the next Attorney General, setting up a potential confirmation fight in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Israel and Iran agree to stop strikes for now, voters in four states head to the polls Tuesday for primaries, Trump makes baseless claims about election fraud in California.
item.content:encoded: Israel and Iran agree to stop strikes for now, voters in four states head to the polls Tuesday for primaries, Trump makes baseless claims about election fraud in California.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Comedian Jeff Foxworthy talks about his new special, 'The Joke's on Me'
item.description: Comedian Jeff Foxworthy has a new standup special on Fox Nation. It's called "The Joke's on Me."
item.content:encoded: Comedian Jeff Foxworthy has a new standup special on Fox Nation. It's called "The Joke's on Me."
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: From side gigs to canceling trips, Americans adjust to months of high gas prices
item.description: Months of higher gas prices are taking a toll. We check in on the trade-offs people are making.
item.content:encoded: Months of higher gas prices are taking a toll. We check in on the trade-offs people are making.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: Iran's soccer team sets up home base in Mexico ahead of World Cup
item.description: Iran's soccer team arrived in Tijuana, Mexico, where they received a warm welcome and are now gearing up for cross-border commutes to the U.S. for every World Cup match.
item.content:encoded: Iran's soccer team arrived in Tijuana, Mexico, where they received a warm welcome and are now gearing up for cross-border commutes to the U.S. for every World Cup match.
item.dc:creator: Eyder Peralta

item.title: Pope Leo calls AI firms a new form of colonialism, echoing tech critics
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with tech journalist Karen Hao about the Pope's recent warnings that AI companies represent a new form of colonialism.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with tech journalist Karen Hao about the Pope's recent warnings that AI companies represent a new form of colonialism.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Staff resignations deepen health care problems at New York jail
item.description: A New York jail is struggling to provide adequate health care and pay medical workers, even after the last health vendor went bankrupt and a new one took over. Now, nurses are resigning.
item.content:encoded: A New York jail is struggling to provide adequate health care and pay medical workers, even after the last health vendor went bankrupt and a new one took over. Now, nurses are resigning.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Izar

item.title: Primary voters in 4 states cast their ballots Tuesday
item.description: In Maine, a Senate primary shines light on a tight general election matchup while gubernatorial primaries in South Carolina and Nevada may signal the future for the Republican and Democratic parties.
item.content:encoded: In Maine, a Senate primary shines light on a tight general election matchup while gubernatorial primaries in South Carolina and Nevada may signal the future for the Republican and Democratic parties.
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller
